name: Cerro El Viso — circular desde Zulema-Peñas Albas
region_intro: Hiking around Villalbilla features a landscape characterized by rolling hills and natural park areas, offering diverse terrain for outdoor activities. The region includes notable elevations like Ecce-Homo and Cerro El Viso, providing varied ascents and viewpoints. Trails often traverse natural parks such as Parque Natural Los Cerros, which contributes to the area's green spaces and open vistas. This environment supports a range of hiking experiences, from gentle paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Villalbilla The most popular hiking route is Alcalá La Vieja y cima del Ecce-Homo – circular por el Parque Natural Los Cerros, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route includes the Ecce-Homo peak and explores the Parque Natural Los Cerros. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Ecce Homo — round trip from Alcalá de Henares, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route offers an ascent to Ecce Homo with views over the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Ruta de la Puerta Verde — Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá circular, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Hiking around Villalbilla is defined by rolling hills, natural park landscapes, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Villalbilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Villalbilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villalbilla?
A: There are over 200 hiking routes around Villalbilla, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The area is known for its rolling hills and natural park areas, providing diverse terrain for exploration.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Villalbilla?
A: Yes, Villalbilla offers nearly 100 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These routes often traverse gentle paths through natural park areas, providing an accessible way to enjoy the scenery.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Villalbilla?
A: Many of the trails in Villalbilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306661633/ruta-de-la-puerta-verde-parque-de-los-cerros-de-alcala-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Puerta Verde — Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.2-mile (6.7 km) trail.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Villalbilla?
A: The best time for hiking in Villalbilla is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally cool but still offer opportunities for hiking, especially on clear days.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking options in Villalbilla?
A: Yes, Villalbilla has many routes suitable for families. The region's rolling hills and natural parks offer gentle paths that are enjoyable for all ages. Look for the easier-rated trails, which are often shorter and have less elevation gain.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Villalbilla?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Villalbilla area, especially within natural park areas like Parque Natural Los Cerros. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, especially near wildlife or agricultural zones.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks along the trails?
A: Villalbilla's trails often lead to scenic viewpoints, particularly around elevations like Ecce-Homo and Cerro El Viso. You can also explore notable peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4618274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce-Homo summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecino Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Villalbilla accessible by public transport?
A: While some trailheads might be reachable by local bus services connecting to larger towns like Alcalá de Henares, many routes in Villalbilla are more easily accessed by car. It's advisable to check specific route starting points and local bus schedules if relying on public transport.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Villalbilla?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and in the villages surrounding Villalbilla. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306780688/cerro-el-viso-circular-desde-zulema-penas-albas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro El Viso — circular desde Zulema-Peñas Albas&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking in residential areas or designated spots at the start of the trail.

Q: What natural monuments or points of interest can I explore near Villalbilla?
A: Beyond the hiking trails, you can visit several natural and man-made monuments. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2663351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Europa Monuments in Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt;, the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6115319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villalbilla?
A: The trails in Villalbilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the natural beauty of areas like Parque Natural Los Cerros, and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Villalbilla?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, Villalbilla does offer a few more challenging options for experienced trekkers, with 5 difficult routes available. These typically involve greater elevation changes and longer distances, such as the ascent to Ecce-Homo.
tips: Panoramic views towards Alcalá de Henares and the Parque de los Cerros.
Cerro El Viso or San Juan del Viso or Zulema offers us views of the southern part of Torreón de Ardoz and adjacent to Alcalá de Henares.
From this point we have wide views of Villalbilla, Los Hueros and Torres de la Alameda (described from left to right).

waypoints.name: View from Cerro de El Viso
